breakdown: PR widens boolean state flag semantics in game_state.py to accept LLM-generated numeric booleans (1, '1', 0, '0') in addition to strict True/'true' values, fixing silent level-up stalls. Added comprehensive tests covering accepted/rejected values and edge cases like bool-subclass-of-int. Excellent test coverage with clear rationale in docstrings; minor deduction for minimal type hints on utility functions.
key_changes: Widen _is_state_flag_true to accept int(1) and string '1' in addition to True/'true'
Widen _is_state_flag_false to accept int(0) and string '0' in addition to False/'false'
Add isinstance check to distinguish bool from int (bool is subclass of int in Python)
Reject free-text strings like 'yes'/'no' to maintain safety semantics
Add StateFlagSemanticsTest class with 5 test methods covering true/false accept/reject cases
